finding_results.notes: Roughened the voicemail at line 31 with three distinct breaks in the polished flow: a self-correcting restart ('I am — I'm composing'), a composure break ('I can't — I actually can't with you right now'), and a thread-losing moment ('I haven't even — shit, wait — the Pacheco pick'). Added character-true low register ('dude,' 'goddamn corpse') and a drunker rambling extension ('That's a guy who showed up to the stadium and accidentally ended up on the field'). Roughened the accounting paragraph at line 33 to include drunken repetition ('circled back to the Addison pick a second time because he'd lost track') and a messier sign-off addition ('and honestly your dignity as a human being'). Updated the true-crime imagining at line 85 and Nate's confession paraphrase at line 175 to match the rougher 'goddamn corpse' phrasing. Restored snapshot-present spoken roughness in the Mallick interview scene: the mid-sentence 'dead' swerve at line 157 where Nate catches himself saying the word and tries to steer around it; fractured verbal stumbling at line 161 with false starts and em-dash breaks replacing smoother thesis-speech; interrupted self-defense at line 169; hesitation in the confession at line 175 ('I was — it was a bit'); and pressured present-tense reliving at line 183 ('it was — I'm saying') replacing the more composed recounting of the prior version.

Removed the imagined episode-title/thesis-restatement run after Nate's confession. The chapter now lands on concrete aftermath already present in the scene: the casserole, draft board, unanswered Mia text, darkening lake, and the renewed scratch of Mallick's pen.

pass_summaries.summary: All packet findings were resolved. The chapter keeps the same comic-paranoia engine and confession end state, but the deputy timeline now matches continuity, Nate's interiority escalates through new leverage shifts instead of restating the same pathology, the ambient punctuation is specific to his listening habits, and the em-dash density has been reduced to zero.

Addressed the single finding by roughening the voicemail from a polished stand-up routine into a messier, drunker, more profane riff with audible restarts, self-interruptions, thread-losing, and character-true low register (meeting all acceptance criteria). Propagated the rougher phrasing to the accounting paragraph, the true-crime imagining, and Nate's confession paraphrase. Restored snapshot-present dialogue roughness in the Mallick interview scene — particularly the mid-sentence 'dead' swerve, the fractured verbal stumbling before the 'when I said' slip, and the pressured present-tense reliving in the confession — where the current chapter had drifted into smoother, more thesis-like speech. All must-land beats, state deltas, incriminating death language, and chapter architecture preserved. Heading contract intact.
Trimmed the closing so Nate's exposure lands as an external aftermath image rather than a second-pass self-interpretation.
